description: Auto-discovered from plugin manifest scanned at 2026-04-26T12:40:03.028Z. Review the journey list below: rename product_name, refine feature expectations to match your customer language, drop any surface that is not customer-facing, add anything the scanner missed. The audit method is plugin-agnostic; this list is the contract.

lenses.label: Customer expectation
lenses.prompt: What would a typical site owner / member expect when they need this?

lenses.label: Current reality
lenses.prompt: What actually happens on a fresh install when they try?

lenses.label: Long-term fix
lenses.prompt: What change closes the gap (UX / default / missing feature / doc / nothing)?

severity_rubric.label: works with friction (workarounds exist, but the user has to learn them)
breaks expectation / sends user to support / forces wp-admin trip for a daily-life task (auto-red, even if it works)

journeys.name: First-run + setup
journeys.perspective: Site owner who just activated the plugin

features.name: Plugin activation
features.expectation: Activate the plugin, see a clear "what now?" prompt within 30 seconds

features.name: Setup wizard (Jetonomy Setup)
features.expectation: Wizard produces a working plugin state in <5 minutes; what is still empty after?

features.name: Database tables (38)
features.expectation: All custom tables exist after activation; dbDelta migrations run cleanly on upgrade

journeys.name: Daily use (frontend)
journeys.perspective: A typical site visitor / member using the plugin day-to-day

features.expectation: Renders cleanly on a page; matches editor preview; handles attribute combinations

features.name: Cron / scheduled tasks (5)
features.expectation: Tasks register; admin can see what runs and when; failures are surfaced

features.name: WP-CLI commands (2)
features.expectation: Commands self-document via `wp help`; primary flows reachable from CLI

features.name: Settings (2 options)
features.expectation: Each option has clear copy, sane default, sanitisation callback, and a working "settings saved" round-trip

features.name: buddypress integration
features.expectation: Auto-detects buddypress via class_exists / function_exists; sane defaults; customer doc matches code; no fatal when third-party is deactivated

journeys.name: Data integrity
journeys.perspective: Plugin data at extreme scale, on schema upgrade, and on uninstall

features.name: Extreme-scale reads on 38 custom tables
features.expectation: Every list query has LIMIT or cursor pagination; no all-rows reads on the customer path

features.name: Uninstall cleanup
features.expectation: uninstall.php removes all custom tables, options, capabilities, cron events

features.name: Schema migrations
features.expectation: dbDelta migrations are idempotent; version bump triggers them; failures surface to admin
